title: Fagara pohliana
uses: Fagara pohliana is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various health conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Traditional healers also use Fagara pohliana to treat infections and promote wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal benefits of this plant.

intro: Fagara pohliana, also known as Java pepper or Szechuan pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Fagara pohliana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and limonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
